methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Rain AI is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Gordon Wilson, currently valued at $500M post-money on $146M raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 3 public semiconductor names (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip peers, with immediate comparables including Substrate ($1.0B, Series A), Etched ($5.0B, Series B), and Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the standard 2.0x multiplier step-up from Series A to next round, implying $1.0B valuation from the current $500M base. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $1.0B median, with a range of $1.0B to $5.0B reflecting heterogeneity between Series A and Series B valuations. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Rain AI's revenue is not disclosed, a common constraint with private companies; the 20.3x median P/S across public semiconductor peers therefore remains unutilized.

The triangulated valuation range is $850M to $1.1B, with a median of $1.0B. This implies upside of 7.5% to 22% from current post-money. Key uncertainties include the timing and severity of Series B expansion, capital intensity ahead, and whether Rain AI's technology enables materially different unit economics than peers in the comparables set.
